content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
t 
' 'Practices Commission 
James J. Coughlin 
Coughlin & Schmitt 
51 East Foothill Boulevard 
Arcadia, CA 
91006 
Dear Mr.Coughlin: 
June 15, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 87-152 
You have written seeking advice concerning the duties of 
former Duarte City Manager Ken Caresio under the conflict of 
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").Y 
The Commission provides advice to persons whose duties under 
the Act are in question. 
(Section 83114.) 
We do not provide 
advice relating to past conduct, nor do we provide advice to 
third parties about another person's duties under the Act 
unless that person authorizes the request. 
(Regulation 
18329(b) (8) (A), (B).) 
Your request deals with past conduct, and it does not 
appear that Mr. Caresio has authorized you to request advice. 
Therefore, we are unable to advise you in this matter. 
If you 
believe that the Act has been violated, you may contact our 
Enforcement Division at this address or by telephone at 
(916) 322-6441. 
Sincerely, 
~M.~~ 
Diane M. Griffiths 
in ... /~ 
General Counsel 
- -; 
DMG:plh 
Y 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California 
Administrative Code section 18000, et seq. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California 
Administrative Code. 
428 I Street. Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 9c)R04,OR07 • 
(916 )322,5660 


LAW OFFICES 
COUGHLIN & SCHMITT 
51 EAST FOOTHILL BOU:.....EVA;;;ZD 
..JA'V1ES .....1. 
WILFRED ..J. SCHMITT 
ARCADIA, CALIFORNIA 91006 
OR A 
June 4, 1987 
CALIFORNIA FAIR POLITICAL 
PRACTICES COMMISSION 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 0807 
Gentlemen: 
Please be advised that I am the Acting City Attorney for 
the City of Duarte and its Redevelopment Agency. 
The Council/ 
Board has raised an inquiry regarding a possible conflict of 
interests, and/or unfair political practice by the City's former 
City Manager and Executive Director of said Ag~ncy, Ken Caresio. 
I have been instructed to request an opinion from you, given 
the following facts: 
1. 
Mr. Caresio resigned on January 9, 1987 from the City of 
Duarte, as said City Manager and Executive Director. 
Concurrently, 
with said resignation, and immediately thereafter, Mr. Caresio 
became employed as a Senior Vice President of Chilton and OIConnor, 
an underwriting firm in Los Angeles, California 
The said resigna-
tion of Mr. Caresio severed all employment ties with said City and 
said Agency. 
2. 
On May 19, 1987, at a meeting of the City Council/Community 
Redevelopment Agency, it was resolved to hire Chilton and O'Connor 
as underwriter for the sale of tax allocation bonds to refund tax 
allocation bonds issued in 1978, as part of the City's/Agency's 
amended Davis Addition Project Area. 
On June 2, 1987, said Council 
and Agency Board resolved to sell said bonds, and have executed the 
necessary documents for same, including a purchase contract with 
Chilton and O'Connor, as purchaser of said bonds. 
3. 
From January 10, 1987 to the present, Mr 
Caresio has been an 
employee of said Chilton and O'Connor, as Senior Vice President. 
Would you advise me on behalf of said City and Agency, at your 
earliest convenience, as to whether or not Mr. Caresio has engaged 
in any activity reSUlting in a conflict of interests, or unfair 
political practices, in regard to the given transactions. 
Thank 


CALIFORNIA FAIR POLITICAL 
PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Page 2. 
you for your consideration in this matter. 
JJC:em 
cc: 
Jess Duff, City Manager 
City of Duarte 
Cordially,

sections.conclusion: Your request deals with past conduct, and it does not appear that Mr. Caresio has authorized you to request advice. Therefore, we are unable to advise you in this matter. If you believe that the Act has been violated, you may contact our Enforcement Division at this address or by telephone at (916) 322-6441.
sections.question_synthetic: Does former Duarte City Manager Ken Caresio have a conflict of interest or engage in unfair political practices by joining Chilton & O'Connor as Senior Vice President after resigning, when the city subsequently hired that firm as underwriter for bond sales?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the request concerns past conduct and was not authorized by Mr. Caresio himself, as required by FPPC regulations. The requesting party is directed to contact the Enforcement Division if they believe a violation has occurred.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter, not a full advice letter. The question text was synthesized from the incoming request letter included in the file. The FPPC response includes clear regulatory citations (18329(b)(8)(A), (B)) explaining the basis for declination.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does former Duarte City Manager Ken Caresio have a conflict of interest or engage in unfair political practices by joining Chilton & O'Connor as Senior Vice President after resigning, when the city subsequently hired that firm as underwriter for bond sales?

CONCLUSION: Your request deals with past conduct, and it does not appear that Mr. Caresio has authorized you to request advice. Therefore, we are unable to advise you in this matter. If you believe that the Act has been violated, you may contact our Enforcement Division at this address or by telephone at (916) 322-6441.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice regarding alleged conflict of interest by former Duarte City Manager Ken Caresio, citing that the request concerned past conduct and lacked authorization from the subject individual.
